TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == bahel == bahelť£õ, mapabahelšłćŚįŹŚŅÉť£õŤĶį, mubahelŚú®ť£õ, mubahelayŚú®ť£õÁöĄ siwkay nu bahel bahel sa, u mauzip saca tuutuud talaayaw i tapuku. tinaku ayam atu hikuki. anu ahemaw ku tuutuud, taneng tu mabahel i tapuku, mahiza ku besu. mapabahel mapabahel nu maku ku banul i nasawni. mubahel saliyuliyut sa ku adipapang mubahel i tapuku. mubahelay mubahelay i paduku sa u ayam hang niyam. u sulit nu Hulam atu Lipun Flight is the process by which an object moves, through an atmosphere or beyond it, as in the case of spaceflight. This can be achieved by generating aerodynamic lift, propulsive thrust, aerostatically using buoyancy, or by ballistic movement. Many things fly, from natural aviators such as birds, bats and insects to human inventions such as missiles, aircraft such as airplanes, helicopters and balloons, to rockets such as spacecraft. The engineering aspects of flight are the purview of aerospace engineering which is subdivided into aeronautics, the study of vehicles that travel through the air, and astronautics, the study of vehicles that travel through space, and in ballistics, the study of the flight of projectiles. namakayniay a nasulitan nasakamuan atu natinenganňź
S.PHON: == bańß…ôr~…ĺ == bańß…ôr~…ĺ*, mapabańß…ôr~…ĺ*****, mubańß…ôr~…ĺ**, mubańß…ôr~…ĺaj*** siwkaj nu bańß…ôr~…ĺ bańß…ôr~…ĺ sa, u mauzip sa ¶a tuutuud tar~…ĺaajaw i tapuku. tinaku ajam atu ńßikuki. anu ańß…ômaw ku tuutuud, tan…ôŇč tu mabańß…ôr~…ĺ i tapuku, mańßiza ku b…ôsu. mapabańß…ôr~…ĺ mapabańß…ôr~…ĺ nu maku ku banur~…ĺ i nasawni. mubańß…ôr~…ĺ sar~…ĺijur~…ĺijut sa ku adipapaŇč mubańß…ôr~…ĺ i tapuku. mubańß…ôr~…ĺaj mubańß…ôr~…ĺaj i paduku sa u ajam ńßaŇč nijam. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu r~…ĺipun fr~…ĺigńßt is tńß…ô pro ¶…ôss bj wńßi ¶ńß an obj…ô ¶t mo*…ôs, tńßrougńß an atmospńß…ôr…ô or b…ôond it, as in tńß…ô ¶as…ô of spa ¶…ôfr~…ĺigńßt. tńßis ¶an b…ô a ¶ńßi…ô*…ôd bj g…ôn…ôratiŇč a…ôrodjnami ¶ r~…ĺift, propur~…ĺsi*…ô tńßrust, a…ôrostati ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺj usiŇč buojan ¶j, or bj bar~…ĺr~…ĺisti ¶ mo*…ôm…ônt. manj tńßiŇčs fr~…ĺj, from naturar~…ĺ a*iators su ¶ńß as birds, bats and ins…ô ¶ts to ńßuman in*…ôntions su ¶ńß as missir~…ĺ…ôs, air ¶raft su ¶ńß as airpr~…ĺan…ôs, ńß…ôr~…ĺi ¶opt…ôrs and bar~…ĺr~…ĺoons, to ro ¶k…ôts su ¶ńß as spa ¶…ô ¶raft. tńß…ô …ôŇčin…ô…ôriŇč asp…ô ¶ts of fr~…ĺigńßt ar…ô tńß…ô pur*i…ôw of a…ôrospa ¶…ô …ôŇčin…ô…ôriŇč wńßi ¶ńß is subdi*id…ôd into a…ôronauti ¶s, tńß…ô studj of *…ôńßi ¶r~…ĺ…ôs tńßat tra*…ôr~…ĺ tńßrougńß tńß…ô air, and astronauti ¶s, tńß…ô studj of *…ôńßi ¶r~…ĺ…ôs tńßat tra*…ôr~…ĺ tńßrougńß spa ¶…ô, and in bar~…ĺr~…ĺisti ¶s, tńß…ô studj of tńß…ô fr~…ĺigńßt of proj…ô ¶tir~…ĺ…ôs. namakajniaj a nasur~…ĺitan nasakamuan atu natin…ôŇčan*
S.FORM: == bahel == bahelť£õ, mapabahelšłćŚįŹŚŅÉť£õŤĶį, mubahelŚú®ť£õ, mubahelayŚú®ť£õÁöĄ siwkay nu bahel bahel sa, u mauzip saca tuutuud talaayaw i tapuku. tinaku ayam atu hikuki. anu ahemaw ku tuutuud, taneng tu mabahel i tapuku, mahiza ku besu. mapabahel mapabahel nu maku ku banul i nasawni. mubahel saliyuliyut sa ku adipapang mubahel i tapuku. mubahelay mubahelay i paduku sa u ayam hang niyam. u sulit nu Hulam atu Lipun Flight is the process by which an object moves, through an atmosphere or beyond it, as in the case of spaceflight. This can be achieved by generating aerodynamic lift, propulsive thrust, aerostatically using buoyancy, or by ballistic movement. Many things fly, from natural aviators such as birds, bats and insects to human inventions such as missiles, aircraft such as airplanes, helicopters and balloons, to rockets such as spacecraft. The engineering aspects of flight are the purview of aerospace engineering which is subdivided into aeronautics, the study of vehicles that travel through the air, and astronautics, the study of vehicles that travel through space, and in ballistics, the study of the flight of projectiles. namakayniay a nasulitan nasakamuan atu natinenganňź
